name: libcarp-clan-perl
title: libcarp-clan-perl
summary: Perl enhancement to Carp error logging facilities
description:
Perl enhancement to Carp error logging facilities
Carp::Clan is a Perl module designed to report errors from the perspective
of the caller of a "clan" of modules, similar to "Carp" itself. Instead of
giving it a number of levels to skip on the calling stack, you give it a
pattern to characterize the package names of the "clan" of modules which
shall never be blamed for any error.
.
These modules stick together like a "clan" and any error which occurs will
be blamed on the "outsider" script or modules not belonging to this clan.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libcarp-clan-perl because Perl enhancement to Carp error logging facilities.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libcarp-clan-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libcarp-clan-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libcarp-clan-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
